amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Fatty acid β-oxidation in E. coli degrades long-chain fatty acids to acetyl-CoA for energy production. Fatty acids are activated to acyl-CoA by FadD, then undergo iterative cycles of β-oxidation via FadE (acyl-CoA dehydrogenase), FadB (enoyl-CoA hydratase and 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase), and FadA (thiolase). Each cycle removes 2 carbons as acetyl-CoA and produces FADH2 and NADH. The pathway is regulated by FadR repressor (relieved by acyl-CoA binding) and requires aerobic conditions.

graph TD %% Fatty Acid Entry A[External Fatty Acid] --> B{OR: Chain
Length?} B -->|Long C12-C18| C[Long-Chain FA] B -->|Medium C6-C12| D[Medium-Chain FA] B -->|Short C4-C6| E[Short-Chain FA] %% Uptake C --> F[FadL Outer Membrane Transporter] D --> F E --> G[Passive Diffusion] F --> H(\1) G --> H H --> I[Inner Membrane Transport] I --> J(\1) %% FadR Regulation J --> K[/FadR Repressor Bound to DNA/] K --> L{AND: Acyl-CoA
Present?} L -->|No - Glucose Growth| M[\fadD/fadE/fadBA Repressed/] L -->|Yes - FA Growth| N[Acyl-CoA Binds FadR] N --> O[FadR Releases DNA] O --> P[\fadD/fadE/fadBA Derepressed/] P --> Q[Fad Enzyme Expression] %% Activation Step Q --> R[FadD Acyl-CoA Synthetase] J --> R R --> S{AND: ATP + CoA
Available?} S -->|No| T[No Activation] S -->|Yes| U[Fatty Acid + ATP + CoA] U --> V[Acyl-AMP Intermediate] V --> W[Acyl-CoA + AMP + PPi] W --> X[Activated Fatty Acid] %% β-Oxidation Cycle Entry X --> Y{OR: Saturation
State?} Y -->|Saturated| Z[Enter Normal β-Oxidation] Y -->|Unsaturated| AA[Additional Isomerases Needed] Y -->|Odd-Chain| AB[Special Propionyl-CoA Step] %% Cycle Step 1: Oxidation Z --> AC[FadE Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase] AC --> AD{AND: FAD
Available?} AD -->|No| AE[/Cycle Blocked/] AD -->|Yes| AF[Acyl-CoA → trans-2-Enoyl-CoA] AF --> AG[FADH2 Produced] %% Cycle Step 2: Hydration AF --> AH[FadB Enoyl-CoA Hydratase] AH --> AI[Water Addition] AI --> AJ[L-3-Hydroxyacyl-CoA] %% Cycle Step 3: Oxidation AJ --> AK[FadB 3-Hydroxyacyl-CoA DH] AK --> AL{AND: NAD+
Available?} AL -->|No| AE AL -->|Yes| AM[/3-Ketoacyl-CoA/] AM --> AN[NADH Produced] %% Cycle Step 4: Thiolysis AM --> AO[FadA β-Ketothiolase] AO --> AP["\{AND: CoA-SH
Available?/"]} AP -->|No| AE AP -->|Yes| AQ[Acetyl-CoA + Acyl-CoA n-2] AQ --> AR[Acetyl-CoA Released] AQ --> AS[Shortened Acyl-CoA] %% Cycle Decision AS --> AT{OR: Continue
Oxidation?} AT -->|Chain > C4| AC AT -->|Chain = C4| AU(\1) AU --> AV[Final Cycle] AV --> AW[2 Acetyl-CoA] %% Energy Production AR --> AX[Acetyl-CoA to TCA Cycle] AW --> AX AX --> AY[TCA Cycle Oxidation] AG --> AZ[FADH2 to ETC] AN --> AZ AZ --> BA[Electron Transport Chain] BA --> BB[~2.5 ATP per FADH2] AY --> BC[~10 ATP per Acetyl-CoA] BB --> BD[Total ATP Generation] BC --> BD %% Unsaturated FA Branch AA --> BE[Enoyl-CoA Isomerase] BE --> BF[Double Bond Repositioning] BF --> AH %% Odd-Chain FA AB --> AG[Normal Cycles] AG --> BH[Final Product Propionyl-CoA] BH --> BI[Methylmalonyl-CoA Mutase] BI --> BJ(\1) BJ --> AY %% Oxygen Requirement BA --> BK{AND: O2
Available?} BK -->|No - Anaerobic| BL[/β-Oxidation Blocked/] BK -->|Yes - Aerobic| BD BD --> BM([Energy from Fatty Acids]) M --> BN([Glucose Metabolism]) T --> BO[/(Blocked - No ATP/CoA)/] AE --> BO name: Fatty Acid β-Oxidation and Regulation
notes: β-oxidation shows OR gates for different chain lengths and saturation states.
scientificAccuracy: Based on comprehensive characterization of E. coli Fad system. All enzymes validated.

sources.title: Regulation of fatty acid degradation in Escherichia coli
FadR, a global transcriptional regulator of fatty acid metabolism in Escherichia coli
sources.authors: Zeng, B; Xiong, M; Wang, S; Zheng, S; Wang, X; Cao, X; Song, C; Li, R; Zhang, C; Chen, B; Peng, C; Wang, Z.

sources.title: Genes of the Escherichia coli fatty acid oxidation complex: nucleotide sequence of the fadB gene and its product, a multifunctional enzyme, and genetic organization of the fadAB operon
sources.authors: Nunn, WD; Binstock, JF; Clark, D.
